summary: Successfully tested accuracy display fix and validation features. All requested features are working correctly: accuracy shows actual values (not N/A), chart displays both Loss and Accuracy lines with real data, and validation prevents training when input/output shapes don't match.

design_issues.description: Console shows warnings about chart dimensions but functionality works correctly
design_issues.error_message: The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0

design_issues.issue: WebGL not supported, falling back to CPU backend
design_issues.description: Expected behavior in testing environment - doesn't affect functionality
design_issues.error_message: WebGL is not supported on this device

passed_tests: Training shows accuracy values (0.6407) instead of N/A in metrics box
Training shows loss values (0.9243) instead of N/A in metrics box
Chart displays both Loss line and Accuracy line with actual data points
Validation correctly prevents training when input shapes don't match (784 vs 2)
Clear error message displayed for shape mismatch: 'Input size mismatch! Your first layer expects 784 features, but data has 2'
Default layer configurations updated correctly (Dense: inputSize=2, units=16; Output: inputSize=16, numClasses=3)
Sample data generation works correctly (classification data with input shape [2], 3 classes)
Training panel opens and closes correctly
Training configuration UI functional (epochs, batch size, learning rate, optimizer)
Console debug logging shows proper accuracy calculation with actual values
